questions.question: Why do conditional types like `T extends string ? A : B` fail when used directly in `defineProps`?
options: Vue's compiler resolves types statically and cannot evaluate conditional type branching at compile time
TypeScript does not allow conditional types inside `.vue` files
Conditional types produce union types that Vue's runtime props validator cannot handle
The Vite plugin strips conditional types before passing them to the Vue compiler

questions.question: Which of these type utilities DOES work with type-based `defineProps`?
options: Conditional types (`T extends X ? A : B`)
Ambient global interfaces declared in `.d.ts` files without exports

questions.question: Starting from which Vue version can `defineProps` accept imported interfaces from external files?
